steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks what both texts together support about the two clades. Text 1 says Grande Terre split from Gondwana 80 million years ago and some Gondwanan clades may have persisted through refuge on nearby islands during submergence (80–37 million years ago). Text 2 gives crown ages: harvest spiders at 48.9 million years, cockroaches at 2.7 million years. The question is whether each clade's age is consistent with Gondwanan origin.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "The clade of harvest spiders must have originated on Gondwana, but the clade of cockroaches may or may not have.": The harvest spiders' crown age of 48.9 million years places their founder during the submergence period — the refuge hypothesis is the only way to explain their presence, making Gondwanan origin necessary. The cockroaches' crown age of 2.7 million years is far too recent to require Gondwanan ancestry; they could have arrived another way. ✓
  • "Both the clade of harvest spiders and the clade of cockroaches originated elsewhere and colonized Grande Terre.": This applies the same origin story to both, ignoring the very different crown ages. ✗
  • "Neither the clade of harvest spiders nor the clade of cockroaches originated on Gondwana.": Contradicts the texts' argument that older clades like harvest spiders plausibly have Gondwanan ancestry. ✗
  • "The clade of harvest spiders originated before Grande Terre split from Gondwana, whereas the clade of cockroaches originated after the split but before the periodic submergence events began.": Cockroaches' crown age of 2.7 million years is far too recent for this — submergence ended 37 million years ago. ✗
That matches the correct answer — A is correct.
